pitch.title: Scratch
pitch.logline: Driven to the brink of poverty, a fiercely protective mother of seven exploits a mathematical flaw in the state lottery scratch-off system, drawing the dangerous attention of the local mob who wants her formula.
pitch.core_theme: The dangerous lengths a mother will go to protect and provide for her children.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Sarah works three minimum-wage jobs to support her seven children, constantly living on the edge of ruin. A former math prodigy, she discovers a statistical flaw in the state's scratch-off lottery system. She begins buying specific tickets, winning consistently and finally providing a comfortable life for her family.

Act II: Her sudden influx of cash solves her immediate problems but draws the dangerous attention of a local criminal syndicate that uses the same bodegas to launder money. The mob boss realizes Sarah has cracked the algorithm and threatens her children, demanding she generate winning numbers for them. Sarah is forced into a dangerous game of cat-and-mouse, using her winnings to secretly fund a counter-operation to protect her family.

Act III: In a tense, high-stakes climax, Sarah uses her mathematical genius to set a trap, feeding the mob a sequence of tickets that flags the FBI and the lottery commission. She outsmarts the syndicate, secures a safe future for her kids, and walks away clean, realizing her true power isn't luck, but her fierce maternal drive.

beats.description: Sarah works three grueling jobs to feed her seven children but faces imminent eviction.
Desperate for a miracle she buys a single scratch-off ticket and loses but her dormant math prodigy mind notices a repeating serial number pattern.
Sarah gathers discarded tickets from the trash to test her hypothesis therefore discovering a predictable algorithm in the state system.
She pawns her last valuable possession to buy a specific batch of tickets and wins a massive payout therefore securing her family immediate survival.
Sarah systematically hits different bodegas to avoid suspicion but her frequent cash-ins disrupt a local mob money-laundering route.
Mob enforcers track her movements and corner her in a dark alley demanding to know how she always wins.
The mob boss discovers her identity and visits her home therefore threatening her seven children if she refuses to generate winning numbers for his syndicate.
Sarah agrees to work for the mob but secretly opens a hidden offshore account using a portion of her winnings to fund a counter-operation.
Sarah plays a dangerous game of cat-and-mouse providing just enough winning tickets to satisfy the mob while hiring a private investigator to map the syndicate structure.
A paranoid mob lieutenant catches Sarah buying off-book tickets therefore forcing her to manipulate the numbers to frame the lieutenant as a thief to save herself.
The mob boss executes the framed lieutenant but demands an unprecedented multi-million dollar score by the end of the week.
Sarah realizes the mob will kill her once she delivers the final score therefore she finalizes a mathematical trap that will expose the entire syndicate.
Sarah uses her algorithm to identify a highly specific sequence of tickets guaranteed to flag the FBI and Lottery Commission for systemic fraud.
She delivers the rigged batch of winning tickets to the mob boss therefore initiating the countdown to their destruction.
The mob attempts to cash the massive fraudulent payout but the flagged serial numbers trigger a massive FBI raid capturing the boss and his crew.
Sarah watches the arrests from afar having successfully laundered enough clean money to buy a safe suburban home where she finally rests with her children.

characters.name: Sarah
characters.brief_summary: A brilliant but exhausted mother of seven who abandoned her math prodigy past for family. She transforms from a desperate survivor into a ruthless tactician to protect her children.

psychological_core.wound: Gave up a full-ride MIT scholarship when she got pregnant at eighteen, feeling she sacrificed her potential.
psychological_core.false_belief: I can only be a good mother if I can buy my children's safety and happiness.
psychological_core.fear: Complete destitution and the state taking her children away.
psychological_core.desire: To secure total financial independence for her family.
psychological_core.need: To realize her worth isn't in her wallet, but in her fierce maternal drive and intellect.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in rapid, practical logistics. Constantly calculating time and money. NEVER says 'I need help.'
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Shifts the focus to immediate chores or tasks, asking if homework is done or chores are finished.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Appears as a tired, invisible working-class mom most of the time; her piercing, calculating intellect only flashes when she is observing numbers or cornered.

characters.name: Carmine Falcone
characters.brief_summary: A pragmatic, ruthless local mob boss who treats crime like a corporate enterprise. He sees Sarah not as a person, but as a highly profitable algorithm to be exploited.

psychological_core.wound: Grew up in extreme poverty, watching his father get beaten to death over a minor debt.
psychological_core.false_belief: Control over capital is the only way to prevent victimization.
psychological_core.fear: Unpredictability and losing the structured empire he built.
psychological_core.desire: To completely dominate the city's illicit financial networks.
psychological_core.need: To realize that human desperation cannot be controlled by a ledger.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in calm, corporate metaphors and banking terminology. NEVER raises his voice or makes overt physical threats.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Offers a polite, condescending smile and asks a rhetorical question about the other person's family.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Acts like a friendly neighborhood bank manager or local politician. The sociopathy only leaks out when a number on his ledger does not match his expectations.

characters.name: Leo
characters.brief_summary: Sarah's sixteen-year-old eldest son who acts as the surrogate father to his six siblings. He deeply resents Sarah's sudden influx of dirty money and acts as the B-Story anchor challenging her moral decay.

psychological_core.wound: Had to grow up entirely too fast, losing his childhood to change diapers and cook dinners.
psychological_core.false_belief: My mother's sudden wealth means she is doing something shameful and dangerous that will ruin us.
psychological_core.fear: Becoming an orphan and having his siblings split up in the foster system.
psychological_core.desire: A normal, boring teenage life where he does not have to worry about the rent.
psychological_core.need: To understand and forgive his mother's impossible choices while allowing himself to be a kid.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Uses clipped, overly mature sentences. Sounds more like a tired forty-year-old than a teenager. NEVER asks for anything for himself.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Retreats into silence and physically blocks his younger siblings from view.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Plays the role of a typical moody teen to outsiders, but his hyper-vigilance and protective stance over his siblings reveal his true burden.

characters.name: Mateo
characters.brief_summary: A weary bodega owner who turns a blind eye to the mob's money laundering to keep his store open. He becomes Sarah's unwitting accomplice and moral warning sign.

psychological_core.wound: Lost his brother to gang violence years ago after trying to stand up to the local syndicate.
psychological_core.false_belief: Keeping your head down and ignoring evil is the only way to survive it.
psychological_core.fear: Drawing the mob's attention and losing his livelihood or life.
psychological_core.desire: To run an honest business and retire in peace.
psychological_core.need: To find the courage to take a stand against the parasites ruining his neighborhood.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in hushed, hurried tones, constantly looking over his shoulder. NEVER uses names when talking about the mob.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Pretends to be busy organizing shelves or cleaning the counter when asked a direct question.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Appears as a jovial, friendly shopkeeper to normal customers, but his eyes dart nervously whenever a black SUV drives past his window.

characters.name: Marcus Vance
characters.brief_summary: A disgraced ex-cop turned private investigator whom Sarah hires to map the mob's network. He provides the street-smarts and cynical reality checks she desperately needs to survive the criminal underworld.

psychological_core.wound: Was ousted from the force after his partner framed him for taking cartel bribes.
psychological_core.false_belief: Everyone has a price and no one is actually doing things for the right reasons.
psychological_core.fear: Trusting someone again and being left holding the bag.
psychological_core.desire: To get revenge on the corrupt systems that ruined his life.
psychological_core.need: To believe in genuine, selfless heroism again.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Sarcastic, blunt, and uses dark humor to defuse tension. NEVER offers a compliment without a caveat.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Lights a cigarette or chews a toothpick and makes a morbid joke about the situation.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Projects an aura of lazy indifference, slouching and sighing, but his eyes are constantly assessing exit routes and potential weapons in the room.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the 'ordinary world' deficit that makes the protagonist vulnerable to the 'magic' of the mathematical flaw.
beats.emotional_arc: Overwhelming exhaustion and deep maternal inadequacy.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, grinding, and claustrophobic to emphasize the crushing weight of poverty.
beats.detailed_action: Sarah scrubs the linoleum floor of a late-night diner, her mind racing through a complex calculus of unpaid bills and overdue rent. She returns to her cramped apartment to find a bright pink eviction notice taped to the door. Inside, her sixteen-year-old son Leo is stretching a single box of macaroni to feed his six younger siblings, highlighting her profound failure to provide.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Foreshadows the central lesson that the 'magic' of sudden wealth cannot replace genuine human connection and safety.
beats.emotional_arc: A painful prick of truth that Sarah defensively ignores.
beats.pacing_notes: A brief, quiet moment of intimacy amidst the chaotic household.
beats.detailed_action: As Sarah desperately tries to reassure her children that everything will be fine, Leo pulls her aside with a harsh dose of reality. He quietly tells her that she cannot simply out-work or out-calculate systemic poverty, challenging her belief that money is the only measure of a good mother. This brief exchange plants the thematic seed regarding the true nature of maternal protection and the limits of financial salvation.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Demonstrates the protagonist's hidden 'magic wand' (her math genius) long before she figures out how to use it.
beats.emotional_arc: Frustration mixed with a simmering, dormant pride.
beats.pacing_notes: Methodical and repetitive, building tension through the sheer unsustainability of her lifestyle.
beats.detailed_action: The crushing weight of Sarah's daily existence is put on full display as she shuttles between three minimum-wage jobs while running on fumes. Flashes of her past brilliance are shown when she effortlessly corrects a complex accounting error at her day job, only to be reprimanded for overstepping. She is completely invisible to the world, a dormant MIT prodigy trapped in a cycle of poverty, terrified that the state will soon intervene and separate her children.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The discovery of the 'spell'—the mathematical flaw that promises to solve all her problems.
beats.emotional_arc: A sudden jolt of adrenaline and dangerous curiosity.
beats.pacing_notes: A sharp pivot from slow drudgery to rapid, hyper-focused intellectual energy.
beats.detailed_action: Driven to absolute desperation by the looming eviction, Sarah breaks her own strict rules and buys a single scratch-off ticket at Mateo's bodega. She loses the game, but as she stares at the seemingly random serial numbers on the back of the ticket, her dormant mathematical genius suddenly flares to life. She recognizes a distinct, repeating sequence within the state's printing algorithm, presenting a dangerous but irresistible glimmer of hope.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist questions if she should use the 'magic', knowing it borders on illicit behavior.
beats.emotional_arc: Intense internal conflict between moral rectitude and desperate survival.
beats.pacing_notes: Tense and obsessive, characterized by sleepless nights and frantic calculations.
beats.detailed_action: Unwilling to trust her initial instinct, Sarah spends the night digging through the bodega's dumpster to collect hundreds of discarded, losing tickets. She covers her kitchen table in complex mathematical proofs, desperately testing her hypothesis against the physical evidence. The agonizing question arises of whether she should pawn her deceased mother's wedding ring, her absolute last asset, to buy the specific batch of tickets required to prove her theory.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist actively uses the 'magic' to cross into the upside-down world of sudden wealth.
beats.emotional_arc: Terrifying commitment yielding explosive, euphoric relief.
beats.pacing_notes: A fast, decisive sequence of actions culminating in a high-energy victory.
beats.detailed_action: Making a definitive choice to leave her passive life behind, Sarah pawns the heirloom ring and returns to Mateo's bodega with a singular focus. She purchases a highly specific, mathematically verified batch of scratch-off tickets and immediately scratches them to reveal a massive, life-altering payout. This decisive action secures her family's immediate survival but permanently crosses her into a treacherous new world of sudden wealth and hidden dangers.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the moral counterweight to the 'magic'—Leo's suspicion grounds the fantasy in harsh reality.
beats.emotional_arc: Defensive guilt clashing with teenage betrayal.
beats.pacing_notes: A dramatic slowdown to focus on character dynamics and emotional fallout.
beats.detailed_action: Returning home with bags of fresh groceries and new clothes, Sarah is met not with pure joy, but with Leo's deep, protective suspicion. He discovers the thick stacks of cash hidden in her bedroom and confronts her, terrified that she has resorted to drug running or theft. This escalating tension between mother and son anchors the emotional core of the narrative, serving as a constant moral compass as Sarah wades deeper into illicit waters.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The 'promise of the premise' where the magic works perfectly, right before the curse of the bottle takes effect.
beats.emotional_arc: Exhilarating triumph that slowly rots into mounting paranoia.
beats.pacing_notes: Upbeat, rhythmic montage of success that abruptly grinds to a halt.
beats.detailed_action: Sarah begins a systematic, highly calculated tour of the city's bodegas, hitting different locations to avoid suspicion while raking in consistent winnings. She experiences a brief, euphoric period of wish-fulfillment as she pays off debts, buys her kids whatever they want, and finally breathes easily. However, her frequent cash-ins unwittingly disrupt a massive money-laundering route controlled by local mob boss Carmine Falcone, drawing the worst kind of attention.

beats.genre_variation_notes: A false victory/defeat where the 'magic' attracts the monster; the wish fulfillment becomes a deadly curse.
beats.emotional_arc: Absolute terror masked by cold, calculated compliance.
beats.pacing_notes: A massive spike in tension, shifting the genre from a caper to a life-or-death thriller.
beats.detailed_action: The illusion of safety shatters when Falcone's enforcers track Sarah's movements and corner her in a dark alley behind a bodega. Falcone himself pays a terrifying, polite visit to her apartment, subtly threatening her seven children if she refuses to generate winning numbers for his syndicate. Sarah feigns complete submission and agrees to work for the mob, but secretly uses her winnings to hire a disgraced private investigator, Marcus Vance, to map the syndicate's vulnerabilities.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The complications of using the 'magic' for evil multiply as both the mob and the law become threats.
beats.emotional_arc: Suffocating pressure and the exhausting strain of living a double life.
beats.pacing_notes: Relentless, tightening rhythm with no room to breathe.
beats.detailed_action: A deadly game of cat-and-mouse ensues as Sarah provides just enough winning tickets to satisfy Falcone while Marcus Vance digs into the mob's financial architecture. The pressure mounts from all sides as a paranoid mob lieutenant begins tailing Sarah, catching her buying off-book tickets for her own escape fund. She is forced to constantly manipulate the numbers and her own schedule, living in a state of hyper-vigilance as the syndicate's grip tightens around her family.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The 'magic' forces the protagonist to cross an unforgivable moral line, resulting in a whiff of death.
beats.emotional_arc: Horror, profound guilt, and the realization of impending doom.
beats.pacing_notes: A shocking, violent burst of action followed by a stunned, hollow silence.
beats.detailed_action: Backed into a lethal corner by the suspicious lieutenant, Sarah uses her intimate knowledge of the mob's ledger to frame him for stealing syndicate funds. Falcone ruthlessly executes the framed man as a warning, then demands Sarah produce an unprecedented, multi-million dollar score by the end of the week. The visceral shock of being complicit in a murder strips away Sarah's remaining illusions, leaving her with the horrifying realization that Falcone will kill her the moment she delivers the final payout.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist realizes the 'magic' is a curse and she must rely on her own innate strength to survive.
beats.emotional_arc: Total emotional collapse followed by a serene, chilling acceptance.
beats.detailed_action: Sarah collapses on her kitchen floor in the dead of night, overwhelmed by the monstrous compromises she has made in the name of motherhood. Leo finds her weeping and, instead of judging her, offers a moment of profound grace and solidarity, reminding her of the brilliant, resilient woman she truly is. This emotional catharsis allows Sarah to shed her false belief that money alone equals safety, realizing she must weaponize her intellect to destroy the threat completely.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist decides to use the 'magic' one last time, not for personal gain, but to destroy the monster.
beats.pacing_notes: Energetic, precise, and highly structured as the trap is laid.
beats.detailed_action: Armed with a renewed sense of purpose and moral clarity, Sarah meets with Marcus Vance to finalize a daring, mathematically perfect trap. She stops playing defense and actively synthesizes her algorithm to identify a highly specific sequence of tickets guaranteed to flag the FBI and Lottery Commission for systemic fraud. She fully commits to a high-stakes offensive strategy, ready to risk everything to sever the mob's control over her family once and for all.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The ultimate test where the protagonist outsmarts the monster by turning the 'magic' against them.
beats.emotional_arc: Nerve-wracking tension resolving into triumphant vindication.
beats.pacing_notes: Explosive, ticking-clock pacing that culminates in a massive set-piece.
beats.detailed_action: Sarah calmly delivers the rigged, multi-million dollar batch of winning tickets to Falcone, looking the ruthless mob boss dead in the eye without flinching. When Falcone's crew attempts to cash the massive fraudulent payout, the flagged serial numbers instantly trigger a catastrophic system alert. A massive, pre-coordinated FBI raid descends upon the syndicate's headquarters, capturing Falcone and his entire crew while Sarah slips away in the ensuing chaos.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Proof that the protagonist has survived the 'bottle' and emerged transformed, no longer reliant on the magic.
beats.emotional_arc: Deep, enduring peace and maternal fulfillment.
beats.detailed_action: Months later, Sarah stands on the porch of a quiet, safe suburban home, watching her seven children play freely in a spacious backyard. She has successfully laundered enough clean money to secure their future, but more importantly, she is finally present and at peace. The exhausted, invisible woman from the opening is gone, replaced by a formidable matriarch who outsmarted the underworld and reclaimed her family's destiny.
